stories.title: Attorney says man shot by ICE was acquitted, had no warrant
stories.description: The lawyer for Carlos Ivan Mendoza Hernandez presented Salvadoran court documents contradicting DHS claims that his client was a wanted gang member.

stories.title: Artemis II crew to hold press conference from deep space tonight
stories.description: The four astronauts are coasting back toward Earth after a record-breaking lunar flyby and will take media questions at 9:45 p.m. EDT.

stories.title: UAE demands Iran pay reparations after 40 days of attacks
stories.description: Abu Dhabi seeks clarity on the U.S.-Iran ceasefire terms while declaring victory after intercepting thousands of missiles and drones.

stories.title: UK vows to restore shipping confidence in Strait of Hormuz after US-Iran ceasefire
stories.description: PM Starmer arrived in Saudi Arabia to reinforce the fragile two-week truce as Foreign Secretary Cooper urged Iran to halt all threats to commercial shipping.

stories.title: 'Ketamine Queen' sentenced to 15 years in Matthew Perry's death
stories.description: Jasveen Sangha, who sold the fatal dose of ketamine to the "Friends" star, apologized for her "hand in this tragic outcome" before sentencing.

stories.title: Democrats to force Iran war powers votes in both chambers
stories.description: Schumer and Jeffries announced plans to push war powers resolutions next week, with House Democrats also seeking a vote Thursday via unanimous consent.

stories.title: Vance to lead US delegation in Iran talks in Islamabad
stories.description: The first direct negotiations since the war began follow a two-week ceasefire, with Iran's parliament speaker heading Tehran's team.

stories.title: Fed minutes reveal growing caution over Iran war, inflation
stories.description: Wednesday's release showed policymakers increasingly wary of cutting rates as oil prices and geopolitical risks cloud the economic outlook.

stories.title: Zeldin tells climate skeptics to 'celebrate' endangerment finding repeal
stories.description: The EPA chief defended his agency's rollback of the foundational climate rule at a Heartland Institute conference in Washington on Wednesday.

stories.title: UN envoy arrives in Iran amid fragile US ceasefire
stories.description: Jean Arnault's visit follows a two-week US-Iran ceasefire brokered Tuesday with Pakistan's help as peace talks loom Friday in Islamabad.

stories.title: NATO chief Rutte meets Trump amid US threat to leave alliance
stories.description: The White House meeting Wednesday comes as European nations block US base and airspace access for the Iran war, deepening the transatlantic rift.

stories.title: Iran recloses Strait of Hormuz, threatening US ceasefire
stories.description: Tehran shut the vital waterway Wednesday, citing Israeli strikes on Lebanon, as the White House called the move "completely unacceptable."

stories.title: Iran faces economic collapse after 40 days of war
stories.description: A two-week ceasefire halted U.S.-Israeli bombardment, but Reuters reports Iran is "near the brink of economic collapse" with GDP expected to contract sharply.

stories.title: Meta unveils Muse Spark, its first AI model from superintelligence team
stories.description: The closed-source model now powers Meta AI in the U.S., with plans to expand across Instagram, WhatsApp, Facebook, and smart glasses.

stories.title: Jeff Shell fired as Paramount president after leak allegations
stories.description: Shell's ouster follows a $150 million lawsuit accusing him of leaking confidential deal information to a high-stakes gambler.

stories.title: Israel learned of Iran cease-fire deal at last minute
stories.description: Netanyahu was informed by Trump only moments before the two-week truce was announced, and Israel was not part of the negotiations, according to the Journal.

stories.title: DOJ says fired AG Bondi won't appear for Epstein deposition
stories.description: The department argues her subpoena was tied to a role she no longer holds, but the Oversight Committee says it will pursue her testimony.

stories.title: IRGC takes de facto control of Iran as supreme leader reportedly incapacitated
stories.description: Multiple intelligence sources indicate Mojtaba Khamenei is unconscious in Qom, unable to govern, as a military council runs the country.

stories.title: Russia pushes three lunar missions to 2032-2036
stories.description: The delays were announced the same day NASA's Artemis II crew completed a historic moon flyby, the first crewed lunar flight since 1972.

stories.title: NYT names Adam Back as likely Bitcoin creator Satoshi Nakamoto
stories.description: A yearlong investigation built a circumstantial case using writing analysis and timeline gaps, but Back denied being Satoshi more than six times.

stories.title: Hegseth declares US victory as Iran ceasefire takes effect
stories.description: Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire Tuesday night after a 38-day air campaign, contingent on Iran reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: SK Hynix jumps 15% as Samsung's record forecast lifts chip stocks
stories.description: Samsung's preliminary Q1 operating profit of 57.2 trillion won, an eightfold annual increase, fueled a broader rally in Korean chipmakers.

stories.title: Starmer heads to Gulf to back US-Iran ceasefire
stories.description: The prime minister aims to ensure the Strait of Hormuz reopens permanently after a two-week ceasefire was reached overnight.

stories.title: Iran ceasefire reinforces view US emerged weakened, Bloomberg reports
stories.description: Allies and adversaries see the 40-day campaign as a strategic setback that bolstered China and Russia while draining American military and diplomatic capital.

stories.title: IATA chief warns jet fuel recovery will take months despite ceasefire
stories.description: Willie Walsh said Strait of Hormuz disruptions damaged Middle East refining capacity, meaning supply cannot quickly rebound even after a U.S.-Iran ceasefire.

stories.title: U.S. rebuffs Ford's request for aluminum tariff relief
stories.description: The denial comes as fires at a key Novelis plant have disrupted 40% of U.S. auto aluminum supply since September 2025, according to The Wall Street Journal.

stories.title: Amsterdam researchers build metamaterials that learn to change shape
stories.description: Worm-like synthetic materials can learn, forget, and relearn new shapes without a central processor, mimicking living systems.

stories.title: China helped push Iran to accept ceasefire, AP reports
stories.description: Beijing urged Tehran to agree to the two-week truce brokered by Pakistan, as peace talks are set to begin Friday in Islamabad.

stories.title: Judge preserves abortion pill mail access, warns FDA review can't wait
stories.description: A Louisiana federal judge declined to block mifepristone prescriptions by mail but signaled the state could eventually prevail.

stories.title: Fuller wins Georgia runoff to replace Greene by narrow margin
stories.description: Trump-backed Republican Clay Fuller defeated Democrat Shawn Harris by roughly 4 points in one of the most conservative districts in the country.

stories.title: US, Israel strike Iran's largest aluminum plant
stories.description: Strikes on IRALCO in Arak sparked a major fire as Trump warned "a whole civilization will die tonight" if Iran doesn't reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Appeals panel signals 'Alligator Alcatraz' can stay open
stories.description: Judges on the 11th Circuit questioned whether the Florida ICE facility qualifies as a federal action requiring environmental review.

stories.title: House Freedom Caucus breaks with GOP plan to end DHS shutdown
stories.description: Hard-line conservatives rejected the two-track funding strategy, demanding full DHS funding in a single reconciliation bill as the shutdown hits day 52.

stories.title: Kuwait orders residents indoors as Trump's Iran deadline looms
stories.description: Kuwait's Interior Ministry urged citizens and residents to stay home overnight as Trump's 8 p.m. ET deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz approaches.

stories.title: CIA used secret 'Ghost Murmur' tool to find airman in Iran
stories.description: The never-before-deployed technology uses quantum magnetometry to detect a human heartbeat, pinpointing the injured crew member hiding in a mountain crevice.

stories.title: Nearly 1,000 migrants died in Mediterranean in 2026 so far, IOM says
stories.description: The UN migration agency called it one of the deadliest starts to a year since it began tracking data in 2014, with deaths up over 150% in the central Mediterranean.

stories.title: Acting AG Blanche says Trump has 'right' to influence DOJ probes
stories.description: In his first press conference, Blanche also announced a new National Fraud Enforcement Division and denied targeting Trump's political enemies.

stories.title: ICE arrested 800+ people using TSA traveler data
stories.description: Internal ICE records reviewed by Reuters reveal the scope of airport-based immigration enforcement far exceeds prior public estimates.

stories.title: Vance says he's 'here to help' Orban win Hungary election
stories.description: The vice president accused the EU and Ukrainian intelligence of meddling in Hungary's April 12 vote, offering no evidence for either claim.

stories.title: UK, Canada push back on Trump's Iran threats as 8 p.m. deadline nears
stories.description: Downing Street signaled it won't allow U.S. use of British bases for infrastructure strikes, while Carney urged respect for international law.

stories.title: UK bars Ye from entering country, Wireless Festival canceled
stories.description: The Home Office denied the rapper's travel authorization over antisemitism concerns, and organizers scrapped the three-day London event entirely.

stories.title: Turkish drillship arrives in Somali waters for historic first offshore oil drilling
stories.description: The Çağrı Bey will target the Curad-1 well, roughly 370 km off the coast, in a region estimated to hold up to 40 billion barrels of oil and gas.

stories.title: US strikes Kharg Island again as war with Iran intensifies
stories.description: Trump warned "a whole civilization will die tonight" if Iran doesn't agree to a deal, as airstrikes pounded Tehran and missiles hit Saudi Arabia.

stories.title: Tehran's Rafi-Nia Synagogue destroyed in US-Israeli strikes
stories.description: Iran's Jewish community condemned the destruction after overnight airstrikes leveled the historic synagogue, burying Torah scrolls under rubble.

stories.title: Wall Street firm sends analyst into Strait of Hormuz by speedboat
stories.description: Citrini Research dispatched a field analyst with $15,000 in cash and hidden recording gear to observe Iranian shipping controls firsthand.

stories.title: Trump lawyers invoke immunity ruling to claim sweeping executive power
stories.description: An ABC News review found the administration cited the 2024 decision at least 21 times in emergency appeals to expand presidential authority.

stories.title: IEA chief says Middle East war will accelerate renewables shift
stories.description: Fatih Birol told Le Figaro the energy crisis is worse than those of 1973, 1979 and 2022 combined but will transform global energy geopolitics.

stories.title: Leaked transcript shows Orbán offered to serve Putin
stories.description: Bloomberg published a Hungarian government transcript of an October call in which Orbán told Putin, "I am at your service."

stories.title: Deere agrees to $99M settlement and digital tools in right-to-repair suit
stories.description: The proposed deal also commits the company to providing farmers digital repair tools for a decade. A separate FTC lawsuit remains active.

stories.title: Iran war energy shock accelerates global return to nuclear power
stories.description: Countries across Asia are racing to restart reactors and build new plants as the Middle East conflict disrupts roughly one-fifth of global LNG supply.

stories.title: SpaceX details record IPO plans, eyes June roadshow
stories.description: CFO Bret Johnsen told bankers Monday that retail investors will get "a bigger part than any IPO in history," with a June 11 event for 1,500 individual investors.

stories.title: Federal judge upholds Dugan's ICE obstruction conviction
stories.description: U.S. District Judge Lynn Adelman rejected the former Milwaukee County judge's bid for acquittal or a new trial, clearing the way for sentencing and a 7th Circuit appeal.

stories.title: Iran-launched drone kills couple in Erbil village
stories.description: The Peshmerga ministry warned it may abandon restraint if Baghdad fails to act against militias behind the escalating aerial campaign.

stories.title: Iran launches fresh missile barrage at southern Israel
stories.description: Coordinated attacks from Iran, Yemen, and Hezbollah mark a new multi-front escalation as the war enters its sixth week.

stories.title: Israel orders evacuation of 41 towns in southern Lebanon
stories.description: The Monday evening directive expands the buffer zone to the Zahrani River as airstrikes intensify across Lebanon, killing dozens over the weekend.

stories.title: Iranian drone strike wounds 15 US troops at Kuwait air base
stories.description: CBS News reports most of the injured service members at Ali Al Salem Air Base have returned to duty, as the US casualty toll in the Iran war rises to 373 wounded.

stories.title: USS Murtha departs San Diego to recover Artemis II crew
stories.description: The Navy recovery ship set sail Monday as the four astronauts performed their lunar flyby, with splashdown set for Friday.

stories.title: Saudi Arabia intercepts missiles as debris falls near energy sites
stories.description: Up to 11 ballistic missiles were fired at the kingdom's oil-rich Eastern Province, with damage assessment ongoing at undisclosed energy facilities.

stories.title: Four IDF combat soldiers arrested for suspected Iran espionage
stories.description: The arrests mark one of the most serious spy cases since the war began, with two other Iran-linked espionage affairs revealed the same day.

stories.title: Minneapolis releases video contradicting ICE's account of January shooting
stories.description: Footage shows a 12-second scuffle, not the three-minute assault with a shovel and broom that officers described under oath.

stories.title: Rep. Ansari moves to impeach Hegseth over Iran war
stories.description: The Arizona Democrat, the only Iranian American in Congress, accused the defense secretary of war crimes and reckless endangerment of U.S. troops.

stories.title: UN issues urgent call for aid as Cuba crisis worsens
stories.description: Blackouts exceeding 18 hours a day affect millions as the UN unveils an updated plan to support two million people across eight provinces.

stories.title: Israeli strikes kill at least 10 near Gaza school
stories.description: A WHO driver was also killed in a separate attack, prompting the suspension of all medical evacuations from Gaza via Rafah.

stories.title: HHS rewrites vaccine panel rules after court blocked Kennedy's appointees
stories.description: The new charter drops expertise requirements for ACIP members, potentially letting Kennedy bypass a judge's finding that his picks were unqualified.

stories.title: OpenAI urges state AGs to probe Musk ahead of April trial
stories.description: The company accused Musk of waging a destabilization campaign, including allegedly coordinating with Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg.

stories.title: Education Dept. terminates agreements protecting trans students at six schools
stories.description: The department ended Title IX settlements with five school districts and a college that required protections for transgender students from discrimination.

stories.title: Lutnick set for May 6 House interview on Epstein ties
stories.description: The commerce secretary's voluntary transcribed interview before the Oversight Committee follows months of bipartisan pressure over his connections to the late sex offender.

stories.title: US-Israeli strikes destroy AI data center at Tehran university
stories.description: The predawn attack reduced Sharif University of Technology's ICT building to rubble, also damaging labs, a mosque, and faculty buildings.

stories.title: Appeals court bars New Jersey from regulating Kalshi
stories.description: The Third Circuit ruled 2-1 that federal commodities law preempts state gambling enforcement, marking Kalshi's first appellate victory.

stories.title: White House proposes cutting 9,400 TSA jobs, $1.5B from budget
stories.description: The plan would shrink the post-9/11 agency's workforce by roughly 16% and reduce its $7.8 billion budget by about 20%, according to budget documents.

stories.title: Study of 1,700 languages finds shared grammar rules
stories.description: About one-third of long-proposed "linguistic universals" hold up under modern statistical testing, pointing to deep cognitive forces shaping all human languages.

stories.title: Supreme Court vacates Bannon's contempt-of-Congress conviction
stories.description: The justices returned the case to a lower court where prosecutors have already moved to dismiss the indictment tied to Bannon's Jan. 6 subpoena defiance.

stories.title: Trump threatens to jail journalist over Iran F-15 rescue leak
stories.description: The president claims media reporting on the downed jet tipped off Iran and endangered the wounded airman's rescue behind enemy lines.

stories.title: Democrats call US fuel blockade on Cuba 'economic bombing'
stories.description: Reps. Jayapal and Jackson described hospital blackouts, food shortages, and preventable deaths after a five-day congressional visit to the island.

stories.title: Iran accuses IAEA of inaction over Bushehr strikes
stories.description: Tehran says the UN nuclear watchdog's silence "emboldens aggression" after a fourth U.S.-Israeli strike near its only operational nuclear plant.

stories.title: Treasury taps BNY and Robinhood to run Trump Accounts
stories.description: The firms will build a dedicated app and manage government-backed $1,000 investment accounts for millions of newborns ahead of a July launch.

stories.title: OpenAI CFO questions Altman's push for late 2026 IPO
stories.description: Sarah Friar has warned internally that OpenAI may not be ready to go public and has raised doubts about its $600 billion spending plan.

stories.title: Israel hits Iran's largest petrochemical plant as war escalates
stories.description: At least 34 killed Monday as U.S.-Israeli strikes target South Pars complex, universities, and residential areas across Iran.

stories.title: Trump vows to 'obliterate' Iran at White House Easter Egg Roll
stories.description: The president used his first public appearance since hospitalization rumors to field questions on the war, telling reporters Tehran "will say uncle."

stories.title: Gulf funds near $24B deal to back Paramount's Warner takeover
stories.description: Saudi Arabia's PIF, Qatar, and Abu Dhabi locked in equity stakes to back the $81 billion acquisition, the Wall Street Journal reported Sunday.

stories.title: NYC business leaders warn of corporate exodus over Mamdani's tax hike plans
stories.description: The Partnership for the City of New York says major firms are eyeing relocations to lower-cost states as the mayor pushes billions in new taxes.

stories.title: IRGC intelligence chief killed in US-Israeli strikes that left at least 34 dead
stories.description: Iran confirmed the death of Maj. Gen. Majid Khademi as airstrikes hit Tehran, Qom and other cities on the 38th day of the war.

stories.title: Trump says NATO appeal on Hormuz was a deliberate 'test'
stories.description: The president told ABC News that allies failed his loyalty test, calling NATO a "paper tiger" that Putin does not fear.

stories.title: NASA chief Isaacman sees high odds of finding life beyond Earth
stories.description: Administrator Jared Isaacman made the remarks on CNN as the Artemis II crew nears its historic lunar flyby on Monday.

stories.title: State Dept. condemns 'outrageous' militia attacks on US sites in Iraq
stories.description: Iran-backed groups have launched a wave of drone and missile strikes on American diplomatic and military facilities across Iraq amid the broader US-Iran conflict.

stories.title: Jeffries calls on Johnson to end recess, vote on DHS bill
stories.description: House Democratic leader urges the Speaker to reconvene the chamber as the partial DHS shutdown stretches past 50 days with no vote scheduled.

stories.title: Kaine warns Congress won't 'write a blank check' for $1.5T defense budget
stories.description: The Virginia senator cited recent Pentagon firings and the Iran war as reasons to scrutinize the record military spending request.

stories.title: WHO warns of nuclear accident risk as Iran war deadline looms
stories.description: Trump's 48-hour ultimatum for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz expires Monday, as strikes hit near the Bushehr nuclear plant.

stories.title: Putin's approval hits lowest point since Ukraine invasion
stories.description: Russia's Telegram ban triggered a banking outage and a sharp drop in public trust, with approval falling to pre-invasion levels.

stories.title: California mail ballot rejections quadrupled after USPS cut rural pickups
stories.description: A Los Angeles Times analysis found late-arriving ballots surged in the state's 2025 special election, with rural counties hit hardest by postal changes.

stories.title: Trump says US sent guns to Iranian protesters via Kurds
stories.description: The president told Fox News the weapons were funneled through Kurdish forces, but claimed the Kurds "took the guns" for themselves.

stories.title: Congo agrees to accept US third-country deportees
stories.description: The US-funded arrangement includes facilities near Kinshasa, though the number of deportees Congo will receive was not disclosed.

stories.title: Pope Leo celebrates first Easter with call to 'lay down' weapons
stories.description: The pontiff urged world leaders to choose peace "through dialogue" in his inaugural Urbi et Orbi address before 50,000 people in St. Peter's Square.

stories.title: Moore rejects Trump's push for states to fund Medicare and Medicaid
stories.description: Maryland's governor called Trump's proposal "absurd," citing his state's $62 million SNAP rescue as proof states can't absorb federal programs.

stories.title: Navy SEALs rescue downed F-15E airman from Iran
stories.description: SEAL Team 6 extracted a wounded Air Force colonel who evaded Iranian forces for over 24 hours in mountainous terrain after his jet was shot down.

stories.title: Artemis II crew sees far side of the Moon
stories.description: The four astronauts aboard the Orion spacecraft shared their observations of the lunar far side in a BBC interview on Saturday.

stories.title: Gulf nations mobilize to secure food as Iran war chokes imports
stories.description: The UAE, Qatar, and Jordan are deploying inspections, strategic reserves, and emergency financing to counter Strait of Hormuz disruptions.

stories.title: Trump aides push legal case for striking Iran's civilian infrastructure
stories.description: Defense Secretary Hegseth and senior advisors argued that bridges and power plants are legitimate military targets, but legal experts warn strikes could violate international law.

stories.title: Explosives found near Serbia-Hungary gas pipeline; Orban convenes emergency defense council
stories.description: Serbian authorities discovered suspected explosive devices near TurkStream-linked infrastructure as Hungary's April 12 elections approach.

stories.title: Iran hangs two more protesters as execution wave accelerates
stories.description: The men were convicted of storming a military facility during January's unrest. Rights groups warn more executions are imminent.

stories.title: China builds largest South China Sea island as US forces shift to Iran
stories.description: Beijing's dredging at Antelope Reef has created roughly 1,490 acres of new land since October, potentially its biggest military outpost in the disputed waters.

stories.title: JBS workers end three-week strike, will return Tuesday without a new contract
stories.description: About 3,800 meatpacking workers at the Greeley, Colorado, plant will resume work as negotiations are set for April 9 and 10.

stories.title: US commits nearly all JASSM-ER cruise missiles to Iran war
stories.description: Only 425 of the stealth weapons remain available globally after the Pentagon ordered transfers from Pacific and domestic stockpiles, Bloomberg reported.

stories.title: US wage growth hits slowest pace since 2021 as Iran war stokes inflation fears
stories.description: Annual earnings growth for production workers fell to 3.4% in March, well below the 4.2% inflation rate the OECD projects for 2026.

stories.title: WHO warns of humanitarian crisis as Iran hospitals come under attack
stories.description: WHO has verified over 20 attacks on healthcare in Iran since March 1, as strikes continue to cripple medical infrastructure across the country.

stories.title: Meloni offers to help rebuild Qatar's war-damaged gas infrastructure
stories.description: Italy's PM became the first EU and NATO leader to visit the Gulf since the Iran war began, meeting Qatar's emir to address the energy crisis.

stories.title: Trump proposes slashing science, education in $6.7T budget
stories.description: The fiscal 2027 plan would cut the NSF by 55%, the NIH by 13% and eliminate billions in college access and K-12 programs while boosting defense spending.

stories.title: Russia, Ukraine trade deadly strikes as Zelenskyy meets Erdogan
stories.description: Five people were killed and dozens wounded in a Russian drone strike on a market in Nikopol as Zelenskyy held peace-focused talks in Istanbul.

stories.title: Hezbollah claims 38 operations in 24 hours as war with Israel escalates
stories.description: Rocket salvos, drone strikes, and sniper fire hit targets across northern Israel on Friday, with Kiryat Shmona struck at least seven times.

stories.title: Trump administration appeals to resume White House ballroom work
stories.description: The emergency motion argues the halted $400 million project has left the executive mansion "open and exposed," posing security risks.

stories.title: Zelenskyy, Erdogan agree on new security steps at Istanbul talks
stories.description: Ukraine will share wartime expertise and technology with Turkey as part of a broader cooperation deal reached Saturday.

stories.title: Displaced Lebanese Shiites face sectarian hostility amid war
stories.description: Over 1.1 million people have fled Israeli strikes, but many Shiite families encounter discrimination, inflated rents, and eviction demands in host communities.

stories.title: Iran rapidly repairing bombed missile sites, U.S. intel says
stories.description: Intelligence assessments show Iranian forces restoring struck bunkers within hours, even as Trump projects the war will end in weeks.

stories.title: India confirms Iranian oil purchases amid Hormuz crisis
stories.description: The petroleum ministry said refiners have secured crude and LPG supplies, marking India's first Iranian oil shipment since 2019.

stories.title: ICE arrests Soleimani's niece after Rubio revokes green cards
stories.description: Federal agents detained Hamideh Soleimani Afshar and her daughter in Los Angeles after the secretary of state terminated their permanent resident status.

stories.title: Witkoff and Kushner plan first visit to Kyiv after Easter
stories.description: Ukraine's Budanov says the U.S. envoys are expected to travel to Kyiv after Orthodox Easter on April 12, their first trip to the capital.

stories.title: Thailand probes fuel hoarding as Southeast Asia's energy crisis deepens
stories.description: Five weeks after Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz, Southeast Asian nations face mounting fuel shortages, soaring costs, and emergency rationing measures.

stories.title: Former Carter aide calls US a 'rogue nation' over Iran war
stories.description: Gary Sick, who served on the National Security Council under three presidents, warned the US is "flouting the laws of war" as the conflict enters its sixth week.

stories.title: Judge blocks Trump push for race data from colleges in 17 states
stories.description: A federal judge issued a preliminary injunction barring the administration from compelling public universities to submit seven years of admissions data.

stories.title: Erdogan tells NATO chief Iran war has caused 'geostrategic impasse'
stories.description: The Turkish president urged the international community to intensify efforts to end the conflict during a Saturday phone call with Mark Rutte.

stories.title: Slovak, Hungarian PMs demand EU lift Russia energy sanctions
stories.description: The two leaders cited Europe's deepening energy crisis, driven by the Iran conflict, as reason to restore Russian oil and gas flows immediately.

stories.title: Iran downs 2 US warplanes, undercutting Trump's victory claims
stories.description: The shootdowns mark the first time manned U.S. aircraft have been brought down since the war began, with one crew member still missing.

stories.title: Hamas accuses Israel of fabricating threats to justify Gaza ceasefire violations
stories.description: Since the October ceasefire took effect, Israeli attacks have killed at least 713 Palestinians, according to Gaza's Health Ministry.

stories.title: Interception debris strikes Oracle building in Dubai amid Iran conflict
stories.description: Oracle is among 18 U.S. tech firms that Iran's Revolutionary Guard declared "legitimate targets" in late March.

stories.title: Surcharges hit global commerce as Hormuz crisis deepens
stories.description: Amazon, UPS, FedEx, and USPS are passing rising fuel and shipping costs to sellers and consumers as the conflict's supply chain fallout widens.

stories.title: Iran vows to 'finish' any US ground operation as war nears 6th week
stories.description: A diplomatic source said Tehran would determine the outcome of any American ground incursion, adding that no negotiations are underway.

stories.title: UAE rejects Zarif's peace proposal, demands Iran compensate Gulf states
stories.description: Dr. Anwar Gargash called the former Iranian FM's plan "fundamentally flawed" for ignoring Tehran's missile and drone attacks on Arab neighbors.

stories.title: Trump weighs broader cabinet shake-up as war frustration mounts
stories.description: Lutnick, Gabbard, and Chavez-DeRemer are among those on thin ice as Trump's approval hits a term low amid the Iran conflict.

stories.title: Ukrainian drone strikes kill one in Taganrog, hit chemical plants deep in Russia
stories.description: Overnight attacks targeted southern Russia's port city and industrial sites over 750 km from the front lines, leaving four in critical condition.

stories.title: Five EU finance ministers demand windfall tax on energy firms
stories.description: Germany, Italy, Spain, Portugal, and Austria urged the European Commission to tax energy companies' excess profits from the Iran war price surge.

stories.title: Trump's FY2027 budget boosts defense 42%, flat-funds SSA
stories.description: The $2.2 trillion proposal cuts nondefense spending by $73 billion while avoiding reforms to Social Security and Medicare as insolvency looms.

stories.title: US pulled 1,500 sailors from Bahrain naval base after Iranian strikes
stories.description: Relief groups have distributed over $1 million to evacuated families who fled with little more than backpacks, NPR reported.

stories.title: North Korea razes villages near key missile launch site
stories.description: Satellite imagery shows hundreds of buildings demolished near the Sohae station, pointing to an expansion of the facility amid an accelerating weapons buildup.

stories.title: GOP cracks widen over Iran war as midterms loom
stories.description: Rising gas prices, casualties, and an approaching War Powers Resolution deadline fuel Republican defections from Trump on Iran.

stories.title: US Embassy urges Americans to leave Lebanon over Iran university threats
stories.description: The Beirut embassy warned Friday that Iran and allied militias may target universities in Lebanon amid ongoing airstrikes and rocket fire.

stories.title: French, Japanese ships cross Hormuz in first Western transits since Iran war
stories.description: CMA CGM and Mitsui O.S.K. Lines vessels broke through Iran's selective blockade this week, though roughly 3,000 ships remain stranded globally.

stories.title: Doctors Without Borders confirms Gaza supply shutdown since January
stories.description: Doctors Without Borders warned that Israeli restrictions have left nearly half of essential chronic medications critically low across Gaza.

stories.title: Iran drone strike on Riyadh embassy far worse than disclosed
stories.description: Multiple embassy floors were heavily damaged and sections remain "unrecoverable," according to current and former U.S. officials.

stories.title: Earthquake kills 8 in Afghanistan, tremors felt across South Asia
stories.description: A magnitude 5.9 quake centered in the Hindu Kush region caused a house to collapse on the outskirts of Kabul, killing an entire family.

stories.title: Three charged over arson attack on Jewish ambulances in London
stories.description: The defendants are due to appear at Westminster Magistrates' Court on Saturday after being arrested on April 1.

stories.title: Pentagon logs 365 troops wounded in Operation Epic Fury
stories.description: The Defense Department added the Iran campaign to its official casualty database on Friday, providing the most detailed public accounting of the war's toll.

stories.title: War silences Good Friday in Jerusalem as Pope Leo XIV calls for peace
stories.description: Only a handful of worshippers walked the Via Dolorosa as Israeli restrictions blocked access to holy sites amid the U.S.-Israel war on Iran.

stories.title: Trump signs memo ordering back pay for all DHS employees
stories.description: The directive extends relief beyond TSA workers to roughly 220,000 federal employees who have gone without pay during the 48-day partial shutdown.

stories.title: Justice Alito was secretly hospitalized in March, court confirms
stories.description: The 76-year-old Supreme Court justice was treated for dehydration after falling ill at a Federalist Society dinner in Philadelphia.

stories.title: Oracle faces backlash over H-1B visa filings amid mass layoffs
stories.description: Critics call it a "slap in the face" that the company filed over 3,100 foreign-worker visa petitions in the same period it cut up to 30,000 jobs.

stories.title: Satellite images confirm Iran damaged $136M US radar in Saudi Arabia
stories.description: New imagery shows charring and missing sections on a THAAD radar antenna struck at Prince Sultan Air Base on March 1.

stories.title: 23 state AGs sue to block Trump's mail voting order
stories.description: The coalition filed suit Friday in Boston, arguing the executive order unconstitutionally strips states of their authority to administer elections.

stories.title: IDF says disarming Hezbollah 'not a goal' of Lebanon campaign
stories.description: A senior military official's remarks signal a shift from earlier war aims after one month of fighting that has hit over 3,500 targets.

stories.title: US adds 178,000 jobs in March, far beating forecasts
stories.description: The unemployment rate fell to 4.3% as the labor market rebounded sharply from February's loss of 92,000 jobs.

stories.title: Judge reaffirms block on DOJ subpoenas targeting Fed's Powell
stories.description: The ruling sets up a likely appeal that could delay Trump's push to replace Powell with Kevin Warsh before Powell's term expires May 15.

top.title: Attorney says man shot by ICE was acquitted, had no warrant
top.description: The lawyer for Carlos Ivan Mendoza Hernandez presented Salvadoran court documents contradicting DHS claims that his client was a wanted gang member.

top.title: Artemis II crew to hold press conference from deep space tonight
top.description: The four astronauts are coasting back toward Earth after a record-breaking lunar flyby and will take media questions at 9:45 p.m. EDT.

top.title: UAE demands Iran pay reparations after 40 days of attacks
top.description: Abu Dhabi seeks clarity on the U.S.-Iran ceasefire terms while declaring victory after intercepting thousands of missiles and drones.

top.title: UK vows to restore shipping confidence in Strait of Hormuz after US-Iran ceasefire
top.description: PM Starmer arrived in Saudi Arabia to reinforce the fragile two-week truce as Foreign Secretary Cooper urged Iran to halt all threats to commercial shipping.

top.title: 'Ketamine Queen' sentenced to 15 years in Matthew Perry's death
top.description: Jasveen Sangha, who sold the fatal dose of ketamine to the "Friends" star, apologized for her "hand in this tragic outcome" before sentencing.

top.title: Democrats to force Iran war powers votes in both chambers
top.description: Schumer and Jeffries announced plans to push war powers resolutions next week, with House Democrats also seeking a vote Thursday via unanimous consent.

top.title: Vance to lead US delegation in Iran talks in Islamabad
top.description: The first direct negotiations since the war began follow a two-week ceasefire, with Iran's parliament speaker heading Tehran's team.

top.title: Fed minutes reveal growing caution over Iran war, inflation
top.description: Wednesday's release showed policymakers increasingly wary of cutting rates as oil prices and geopolitical risks cloud the economic outlook.

top.title: Zeldin tells climate skeptics to 'celebrate' endangerment finding repeal
top.description: The EPA chief defended his agency's rollback of the foundational climate rule at a Heartland Institute conference in Washington on Wednesday.

top.title: UN envoy arrives in Iran amid fragile US ceasefire
top.description: Jean Arnault's visit follows a two-week US-Iran ceasefire brokered Tuesday with Pakistan's help as peace talks loom Friday in Islamabad.

top.title: NATO chief Rutte meets Trump amid US threat to leave alliance
top.description: The White House meeting Wednesday comes as European nations block US base and airspace access for the Iran war, deepening the transatlantic rift.

top.title: Iran recloses Strait of Hormuz, threatening US ceasefire
top.description: Tehran shut the vital waterway Wednesday, citing Israeli strikes on Lebanon, as the White House called the move "completely unacceptable."

top.title: Iran faces economic collapse after 40 days of war
top.description: A two-week ceasefire halted U.S.-Israeli bombardment, but Reuters reports Iran is "near the brink of economic collapse" with GDP expected to contract sharply.

top.title: Meta unveils Muse Spark, its first AI model from superintelligence team
top.description: The closed-source model now powers Meta AI in the U.S., with plans to expand across Instagram, WhatsApp, Facebook, and smart glasses.

top.title: Jeff Shell fired as Paramount president after leak allegations
top.description: Shell's ouster follows a $150 million lawsuit accusing him of leaking confidential deal information to a high-stakes gambler.

top.title: Israel learned of Iran cease-fire deal at last minute
top.description: Netanyahu was informed by Trump only moments before the two-week truce was announced, and Israel was not part of the negotiations, according to the Journal.

top.title: DOJ says fired AG Bondi won't appear for Epstein deposition
top.description: The department argues her subpoena was tied to a role she no longer holds, but the Oversight Committee says it will pursue her testimony.

top.title: IRGC takes de facto control of Iran as supreme leader reportedly incapacitated
top.description: Multiple intelligence sources indicate Mojtaba Khamenei is unconscious in Qom, unable to govern, as a military council runs the country.

top.title: Russia pushes three lunar missions to 2032-2036
top.description: The delays were announced the same day NASA's Artemis II crew completed a historic moon flyby, the first crewed lunar flight since 1972.

top.title: NYT names Adam Back as likely Bitcoin creator Satoshi Nakamoto
top.description: A yearlong investigation built a circumstantial case using writing analysis and timeline gaps, but Back denied being Satoshi more than six times.

top.title: Hegseth declares US victory as Iran ceasefire takes effect
top.description: Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire Tuesday night after a 38-day air campaign, contingent on Iran reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

top.title: SK Hynix jumps 15% as Samsung's record forecast lifts chip stocks
top.description: Samsung's preliminary Q1 operating profit of 57.2 trillion won, an eightfold annual increase, fueled a broader rally in Korean chipmakers.

top.title: Starmer heads to Gulf to back US-Iran ceasefire
top.description: The prime minister aims to ensure the Strait of Hormuz reopens permanently after a two-week ceasefire was reached overnight.

top.title: Iran ceasefire reinforces view US emerged weakened, Bloomberg reports
top.description: Allies and adversaries see the 40-day campaign as a strategic setback that bolstered China and Russia while draining American military and diplomatic capital.

top.title: IATA chief warns jet fuel recovery will take months despite ceasefire
top.description: Willie Walsh said Strait of Hormuz disruptions damaged Middle East refining capacity, meaning supply cannot quickly rebound even after a U.S.-Iran ceasefire.

top.title: U.S. rebuffs Ford's request for aluminum tariff relief
top.description: The denial comes as fires at a key Novelis plant have disrupted 40% of U.S. auto aluminum supply since September 2025, according to The Wall Street Journal.

top.title: Amsterdam researchers build metamaterials that learn to change shape
top.description: Worm-like synthetic materials can learn, forget, and relearn new shapes without a central processor, mimicking living systems.

top.title: China helped push Iran to accept ceasefire, AP reports
top.description: Beijing urged Tehran to agree to the two-week truce brokered by Pakistan, as peace talks are set to begin Friday in Islamabad.

top.title: Judge preserves abortion pill mail access, warns FDA review can't wait
top.description: A Louisiana federal judge declined to block mifepristone prescriptions by mail but signaled the state could eventually prevail.

top.title: Fuller wins Georgia runoff to replace Greene by narrow margin
top.description: Trump-backed Republican Clay Fuller defeated Democrat Shawn Harris by roughly 4 points in one of the most conservative districts in the country.

top.title: US, Israel strike Iran's largest aluminum plant
top.description: Strikes on IRALCO in Arak sparked a major fire as Trump warned "a whole civilization will die tonight" if Iran doesn't reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

top.title: Appeals panel signals 'Alligator Alcatraz' can stay open
top.description: Judges on the 11th Circuit questioned whether the Florida ICE facility qualifies as a federal action requiring environmental review.

top.title: House Freedom Caucus breaks with GOP plan to end DHS shutdown
top.description: Hard-line conservatives rejected the two-track funding strategy, demanding full DHS funding in a single reconciliation bill as the shutdown hits day 52.

top.title: Kuwait orders residents indoors as Trump's Iran deadline looms
top.description: Kuwait's Interior Ministry urged citizens and residents to stay home overnight as Trump's 8 p.m. ET deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz approaches.

top.title: CIA used secret 'Ghost Murmur' tool to find airman in Iran
top.description: The never-before-deployed technology uses quantum magnetometry to detect a human heartbeat, pinpointing the injured crew member hiding in a mountain crevice.

top.title: Nearly 1,000 migrants died in Mediterranean in 2026 so far, IOM says
top.description: The UN migration agency called it one of the deadliest starts to a year since it began tracking data in 2014, with deaths up over 150% in the central Mediterranean.

top.title: Acting AG Blanche says Trump has 'right' to influence DOJ probes
top.description: In his first press conference, Blanche also announced a new National Fraud Enforcement Division and denied targeting Trump's political enemies.

top.title: ICE arrested 800+ people using TSA traveler data
top.description: Internal ICE records reviewed by Reuters reveal the scope of airport-based immigration enforcement far exceeds prior public estimates.

top.title: Vance says he's 'here to help' Orban win Hungary election
top.description: The vice president accused the EU and Ukrainian intelligence of meddling in Hungary's April 12 vote, offering no evidence for either claim.

top.title: UK, Canada push back on Trump's Iran threats as 8 p.m. deadline nears
top.description: Downing Street signaled it won't allow U.S. use of British bases for infrastructure strikes, while Carney urged respect for international law.

top.title: UK bars Ye from entering country, Wireless Festival canceled
top.description: The Home Office denied the rapper's travel authorization over antisemitism concerns, and organizers scrapped the three-day London event entirely.

top.title: Turkish drillship arrives in Somali waters for historic first offshore oil drilling
top.description: The Çağrı Bey will target the Curad-1 well, roughly 370 km off the coast, in a region estimated to hold up to 40 billion barrels of oil and gas.

top.title: US strikes Kharg Island again as war with Iran intensifies
top.description: Trump warned "a whole civilization will die tonight" if Iran doesn't agree to a deal, as airstrikes pounded Tehran and missiles hit Saudi Arabia.

top.title: Tehran's Rafi-Nia Synagogue destroyed in US-Israeli strikes
top.description: Iran's Jewish community condemned the destruction after overnight airstrikes leveled the historic synagogue, burying Torah scrolls under rubble.

top.title: Wall Street firm sends analyst into Strait of Hormuz by speedboat
top.description: Citrini Research dispatched a field analyst with $15,000 in cash and hidden recording gear to observe Iranian shipping controls firsthand.

top.title: Trump lawyers invoke immunity ruling to claim sweeping executive power
top.description: An ABC News review found the administration cited the 2024 decision at least 21 times in emergency appeals to expand presidential authority.

top.title: IEA chief says Middle East war will accelerate renewables shift
top.description: Fatih Birol told Le Figaro the energy crisis is worse than those of 1973, 1979 and 2022 combined but will transform global energy geopolitics.

top.title: Leaked transcript shows Orbán offered to serve Putin
top.description: Bloomberg published a Hungarian government transcript of an October call in which Orbán told Putin, "I am at your service."

top.title: Deere agrees to $99M settlement and digital tools in right-to-repair suit
top.description: The proposed deal also commits the company to providing farmers digital repair tools for a decade. A separate FTC lawsuit remains active.

top.title: Iran war energy shock accelerates global return to nuclear power
top.description: Countries across Asia are racing to restart reactors and build new plants as the Middle East conflict disrupts roughly one-fifth of global LNG supply.

top.title: SpaceX details record IPO plans, eyes June roadshow
top.description: CFO Bret Johnsen told bankers Monday that retail investors will get "a bigger part than any IPO in history," with a June 11 event for 1,500 individual investors.

top.title: Federal judge upholds Dugan's ICE obstruction conviction
top.description: U.S. District Judge Lynn Adelman rejected the former Milwaukee County judge's bid for acquittal or a new trial, clearing the way for sentencing and a 7th Circuit appeal.

top.title: Iran-launched drone kills couple in Erbil village
top.description: The Peshmerga ministry warned it may abandon restraint if Baghdad fails to act against militias behind the escalating aerial campaign.

top.title: Iran launches fresh missile barrage at southern Israel
top.description: Coordinated attacks from Iran, Yemen, and Hezbollah mark a new multi-front escalation as the war enters its sixth week.

top.title: Israel orders evacuation of 41 towns in southern Lebanon
top.description: The Monday evening directive expands the buffer zone to the Zahrani River as airstrikes intensify across Lebanon, killing dozens over the weekend.

top.title: Iranian drone strike wounds 15 US troops at Kuwait air base
top.description: CBS News reports most of the injured service members at Ali Al Salem Air Base have returned to duty, as the US casualty toll in the Iran war rises to 373 wounded.

top.title: USS Murtha departs San Diego to recover Artemis II crew
top.description: The Navy recovery ship set sail Monday as the four astronauts performed their lunar flyby, with splashdown set for Friday.

top.title: Saudi Arabia intercepts missiles as debris falls near energy sites
top.description: Up to 11 ballistic missiles were fired at the kingdom's oil-rich Eastern Province, with damage assessment ongoing at undisclosed energy facilities.

top.title: Four IDF combat soldiers arrested for suspected Iran espionage
top.description: The arrests mark one of the most serious spy cases since the war began, with two other Iran-linked espionage affairs revealed the same day.

top.title: Minneapolis releases video contradicting ICE's account of January shooting
top.description: Footage shows a 12-second scuffle, not the three-minute assault with a shovel and broom that officers described under oath.

top.title: Rep. Ansari moves to impeach Hegseth over Iran war
top.description: The Arizona Democrat, the only Iranian American in Congress, accused the defense secretary of war crimes and reckless endangerment of U.S. troops.

top.title: UN issues urgent call for aid as Cuba crisis worsens
top.description: Blackouts exceeding 18 hours a day affect millions as the UN unveils an updated plan to support two million people across eight provinces.

top.title: Israeli strikes kill at least 10 near Gaza school
top.description: A WHO driver was also killed in a separate attack, prompting the suspension of all medical evacuations from Gaza via Rafah.

top.title: HHS rewrites vaccine panel rules after court blocked Kennedy's appointees
top.description: The new charter drops expertise requirements for ACIP members, potentially letting Kennedy bypass a judge's finding that his picks were unqualified.

top.title: OpenAI urges state AGs to probe Musk ahead of April trial
top.description: The company accused Musk of waging a destabilization campaign, including allegedly coordinating with Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg.

top.title: Education Dept. terminates agreements protecting trans students at six schools
top.description: The department ended Title IX settlements with five school districts and a college that required protections for transgender students from discrimination.

top.title: Lutnick set for May 6 House interview on Epstein ties
top.description: The commerce secretary's voluntary transcribed interview before the Oversight Committee follows months of bipartisan pressure over his connections to the late sex offender.

top.title: US-Israeli strikes destroy AI data center at Tehran university
top.description: The predawn attack reduced Sharif University of Technology's ICT building to rubble, also damaging labs, a mosque, and faculty buildings.

top.title: Appeals court bars New Jersey from regulating Kalshi
top.description: The Third Circuit ruled 2-1 that federal commodities law preempts state gambling enforcement, marking Kalshi's first appellate victory.

top.title: White House proposes cutting 9,400 TSA jobs, $1.5B from budget
top.description: The plan would shrink the post-9/11 agency's workforce by roughly 16% and reduce its $7.8 billion budget by about 20%, according to budget documents.

top.title: Study of 1,700 languages finds shared grammar rules
top.description: About one-third of long-proposed "linguistic universals" hold up under modern statistical testing, pointing to deep cognitive forces shaping all human languages.

top.title: Supreme Court vacates Bannon's contempt-of-Congress conviction
top.description: The justices returned the case to a lower court where prosecutors have already moved to dismiss the indictment tied to Bannon's Jan. 6 subpoena defiance.

top.title: Trump threatens to jail journalist over Iran F-15 rescue leak
top.description: The president claims media reporting on the downed jet tipped off Iran and endangered the wounded airman's rescue behind enemy lines.

top.title: Democrats call US fuel blockade on Cuba 'economic bombing'
top.description: Reps. Jayapal and Jackson described hospital blackouts, food shortages, and preventable deaths after a five-day congressional visit to the island.

top.title: Iran accuses IAEA of inaction over Bushehr strikes
top.description: Tehran says the UN nuclear watchdog's silence "emboldens aggression" after a fourth U.S.-Israeli strike near its only operational nuclear plant.

top.title: Treasury taps BNY and Robinhood to run Trump Accounts
top.description: The firms will build a dedicated app and manage government-backed $1,000 investment accounts for millions of newborns ahead of a July launch.

top.title: OpenAI CFO questions Altman's push for late 2026 IPO
top.description: Sarah Friar has warned internally that OpenAI may not be ready to go public and has raised doubts about its $600 billion spending plan.

top.title: Israel hits Iran's largest petrochemical plant as war escalates
top.description: At least 34 killed Monday as U.S.-Israeli strikes target South Pars complex, universities, and residential areas across Iran.

top.title: Trump vows to 'obliterate' Iran at White House Easter Egg Roll
top.description: The president used his first public appearance since hospitalization rumors to field questions on the war, telling reporters Tehran "will say uncle."

top.title: Gulf funds near $24B deal to back Paramount's Warner takeover
top.description: Saudi Arabia's PIF, Qatar, and Abu Dhabi locked in equity stakes to back the $81 billion acquisition, the Wall Street Journal reported Sunday.

top.title: NYC business leaders warn of corporate exodus over Mamdani's tax hike plans
top.description: The Partnership for the City of New York says major firms are eyeing relocations to lower-cost states as the mayor pushes billions in new taxes.

top.title: IRGC intelligence chief killed in US-Israeli strikes that left at least 34 dead
top.description: Iran confirmed the death of Maj. Gen. Majid Khademi as airstrikes hit Tehran, Qom and other cities on the 38th day of the war.

top.title: Trump says NATO appeal on Hormuz was a deliberate 'test'
top.description: The president told ABC News that allies failed his loyalty test, calling NATO a "paper tiger" that Putin does not fear.

top.title: NASA chief Isaacman sees high odds of finding life beyond Earth
top.description: Administrator Jared Isaacman made the remarks on CNN as the Artemis II crew nears its historic lunar flyby on Monday.

top.title: State Dept. condemns 'outrageous' militia attacks on US sites in Iraq
top.description: Iran-backed groups have launched a wave of drone and missile strikes on American diplomatic and military facilities across Iraq amid the broader US-Iran conflict.

top.title: Jeffries calls on Johnson to end recess, vote on DHS bill
top.description: House Democratic leader urges the Speaker to reconvene the chamber as the partial DHS shutdown stretches past 50 days with no vote scheduled.

top.title: Kaine warns Congress won't 'write a blank check' for $1.5T defense budget
top.description: The Virginia senator cited recent Pentagon firings and the Iran war as reasons to scrutinize the record military spending request.

top.title: WHO warns of nuclear accident risk as Iran war deadline looms
top.description: Trump's 48-hour ultimatum for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz expires Monday, as strikes hit near the Bushehr nuclear plant.

top.title: Putin's approval hits lowest point since Ukraine invasion
top.description: Russia's Telegram ban triggered a banking outage and a sharp drop in public trust, with approval falling to pre-invasion levels.

top.title: California mail ballot rejections quadrupled after USPS cut rural pickups
top.description: A Los Angeles Times analysis found late-arriving ballots surged in the state's 2025 special election, with rural counties hit hardest by postal changes.

top.title: Trump says US sent guns to Iranian protesters via Kurds
top.description: The president told Fox News the weapons were funneled through Kurdish forces, but claimed the Kurds "took the guns" for themselves.

top.title: Congo agrees to accept US third-country deportees
top.description: The US-funded arrangement includes facilities near Kinshasa, though the number of deportees Congo will receive was not disclosed.

top.title: Pope Leo celebrates first Easter with call to 'lay down' weapons
top.description: The pontiff urged world leaders to choose peace "through dialogue" in his inaugural Urbi et Orbi address before 50,000 people in St. Peter's Square.

top.title: Moore rejects Trump's push for states to fund Medicare and Medicaid
top.description: Maryland's governor called Trump's proposal "absurd," citing his state's $62 million SNAP rescue as proof states can't absorb federal programs.

top.title: Navy SEALs rescue downed F-15E airman from Iran
top.description: SEAL Team 6 extracted a wounded Air Force colonel who evaded Iranian forces for over 24 hours in mountainous terrain after his jet was shot down.

top.title: Artemis II crew sees far side of the Moon
top.description: The four astronauts aboard the Orion spacecraft shared their observations of the lunar far side in a BBC interview on Saturday.

top.title: Gulf nations mobilize to secure food as Iran war chokes imports
top.description: The UAE, Qatar, and Jordan are deploying inspections, strategic reserves, and emergency financing to counter Strait of Hormuz disruptions.

top.title: Trump aides push legal case for striking Iran's civilian infrastructure
top.description: Defense Secretary Hegseth and senior advisors argued that bridges and power plants are legitimate military targets, but legal experts warn strikes could violate international law.

top.title: Explosives found near Serbia-Hungary gas pipeline; Orban convenes emergency defense council
top.description: Serbian authorities discovered suspected explosive devices near TurkStream-linked infrastructure as Hungary's April 12 elections approach.

top.title: Iran hangs two more protesters as execution wave accelerates
top.description: The men were convicted of storming a military facility during January's unrest. Rights groups warn more executions are imminent.

top.title: China builds largest South China Sea island as US forces shift to Iran
top.description: Beijing's dredging at Antelope Reef has created roughly 1,490 acres of new land since October, potentially its biggest military outpost in the disputed waters.

top.title: JBS workers end three-week strike, will return Tuesday without a new contract
top.description: About 3,800 meatpacking workers at the Greeley, Colorado, plant will resume work as negotiations are set for April 9 and 10.

top.title: US commits nearly all JASSM-ER cruise missiles to Iran war
top.description: Only 425 of the stealth weapons remain available globally after the Pentagon ordered transfers from Pacific and domestic stockpiles, Bloomberg reported.

top.title: US wage growth hits slowest pace since 2021 as Iran war stokes inflation fears
top.description: Annual earnings growth for production workers fell to 3.4% in March, well below the 4.2% inflation rate the OECD projects for 2026.

top.title: WHO warns of humanitarian crisis as Iran hospitals come under attack
top.description: WHO has verified over 20 attacks on healthcare in Iran since March 1, as strikes continue to cripple medical infrastructure across the country.

top.title: Meloni offers to help rebuild Qatar's war-damaged gas infrastructure
top.description: Italy's PM became the first EU and NATO leader to visit the Gulf since the Iran war began, meeting Qatar's emir to address the energy crisis.

top.title: Trump proposes slashing science, education in $6.7T budget
top.description: The fiscal 2027 plan would cut the NSF by 55%, the NIH by 13% and eliminate billions in college access and K-12 programs while boosting defense spending.

top.title: Russia, Ukraine trade deadly strikes as Zelenskyy meets Erdogan
top.description: Five people were killed and dozens wounded in a Russian drone strike on a market in Nikopol as Zelenskyy held peace-focused talks in Istanbul.

top.title: Hezbollah claims 38 operations in 24 hours as war with Israel escalates
top.description: Rocket salvos, drone strikes, and sniper fire hit targets across northern Israel on Friday, with Kiryat Shmona struck at least seven times.

top.title: Trump administration appeals to resume White House ballroom work
top.description: The emergency motion argues the halted $400 million project has left the executive mansion "open and exposed," posing security risks.

top.title: Zelenskyy, Erdogan agree on new security steps at Istanbul talks
top.description: Ukraine will share wartime expertise and technology with Turkey as part of a broader cooperation deal reached Saturday.

top.title: Displaced Lebanese Shiites face sectarian hostility amid war
top.description: Over 1.1 million people have fled Israeli strikes, but many Shiite families encounter discrimination, inflated rents, and eviction demands in host communities.

top.title: Iran rapidly repairing bombed missile sites, U.S. intel says
top.description: Intelligence assessments show Iranian forces restoring struck bunkers within hours, even as Trump projects the war will end in weeks.

top.title: India confirms Iranian oil purchases amid Hormuz crisis
top.description: The petroleum ministry said refiners have secured crude and LPG supplies, marking India's first Iranian oil shipment since 2019.

top.title: ICE arrests Soleimani's niece after Rubio revokes green cards
top.description: Federal agents detained Hamideh Soleimani Afshar and her daughter in Los Angeles after the secretary of state terminated their permanent resident status.

top.title: Witkoff and Kushner plan first visit to Kyiv after Easter
top.description: Ukraine's Budanov says the U.S. envoys are expected to travel to Kyiv after Orthodox Easter on April 12, their first trip to the capital.

top.title: Thailand probes fuel hoarding as Southeast Asia's energy crisis deepens
top.description: Five weeks after Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz, Southeast Asian nations face mounting fuel shortages, soaring costs, and emergency rationing measures.

top.title: Former Carter aide calls US a 'rogue nation' over Iran war
top.description: Gary Sick, who served on the National Security Council under three presidents, warned the US is "flouting the laws of war" as the conflict enters its sixth week.

top.title: Judge blocks Trump push for race data from colleges in 17 states
top.description: A federal judge issued a preliminary injunction barring the administration from compelling public universities to submit seven years of admissions data.

top.title: Erdogan tells NATO chief Iran war has caused 'geostrategic impasse'
top.description: The Turkish president urged the international community to intensify efforts to end the conflict during a Saturday phone call with Mark Rutte.

top.title: Slovak, Hungarian PMs demand EU lift Russia energy sanctions
top.description: The two leaders cited Europe's deepening energy crisis, driven by the Iran conflict, as reason to restore Russian oil and gas flows immediately.

top.title: Iran downs 2 US warplanes, undercutting Trump's victory claims
top.description: The shootdowns mark the first time manned U.S. aircraft have been brought down since the war began, with one crew member still missing.

top.title: Hamas accuses Israel of fabricating threats to justify Gaza ceasefire violations
top.description: Since the October ceasefire took effect, Israeli attacks have killed at least 713 Palestinians, according to Gaza's Health Ministry.

top.title: Interception debris strikes Oracle building in Dubai amid Iran conflict
top.description: Oracle is among 18 U.S. tech firms that Iran's Revolutionary Guard declared "legitimate targets" in late March.

top.title: Surcharges hit global commerce as Hormuz crisis deepens
top.description: Amazon, UPS, FedEx, and USPS are passing rising fuel and shipping costs to sellers and consumers as the conflict's supply chain fallout widens.

top.title: Iran vows to 'finish' any US ground operation as war nears 6th week
top.description: A diplomatic source said Tehran would determine the outcome of any American ground incursion, adding that no negotiations are underway.

top.title: UAE rejects Zarif's peace proposal, demands Iran compensate Gulf states
top.description: Dr. Anwar Gargash called the former Iranian FM's plan "fundamentally flawed" for ignoring Tehran's missile and drone attacks on Arab neighbors.

top.title: Trump weighs broader cabinet shake-up as war frustration mounts
top.description: Lutnick, Gabbard, and Chavez-DeRemer are among those on thin ice as Trump's approval hits a term low amid the Iran conflict.

top.title: Ukrainian drone strikes kill one in Taganrog, hit chemical plants deep in Russia
top.description: Overnight attacks targeted southern Russia's port city and industrial sites over 750 km from the front lines, leaving four in critical condition.

top.title: Five EU finance ministers demand windfall tax on energy firms
top.description: Germany, Italy, Spain, Portugal, and Austria urged the European Commission to tax energy companies' excess profits from the Iran war price surge.

top.title: Trump's FY2027 budget boosts defense 42%, flat-funds SSA
top.description: The $2.2 trillion proposal cuts nondefense spending by $73 billion while avoiding reforms to Social Security and Medicare as insolvency looms.

top.title: US pulled 1,500 sailors from Bahrain naval base after Iranian strikes
top.description: Relief groups have distributed over $1 million to evacuated families who fled with little more than backpacks, NPR reported.

top.title: North Korea razes villages near key missile launch site
top.description: Satellite imagery shows hundreds of buildings demolished near the Sohae station, pointing to an expansion of the facility amid an accelerating weapons buildup.

top.title: GOP cracks widen over Iran war as midterms loom
top.description: Rising gas prices, casualties, and an approaching War Powers Resolution deadline fuel Republican defections from Trump on Iran.

top.title: US Embassy urges Americans to leave Lebanon over Iran university threats
top.description: The Beirut embassy warned Friday that Iran and allied militias may target universities in Lebanon amid ongoing airstrikes and rocket fire.

top.title: French, Japanese ships cross Hormuz in first Western transits since Iran war
top.description: CMA CGM and Mitsui O.S.K. Lines vessels broke through Iran's selective blockade this week, though roughly 3,000 ships remain stranded globally.

top.title: Doctors Without Borders confirms Gaza supply shutdown since January
top.description: Doctors Without Borders warned that Israeli restrictions have left nearly half of essential chronic medications critically low across Gaza.

top.title: Iran drone strike on Riyadh embassy far worse than disclosed
top.description: Multiple embassy floors were heavily damaged and sections remain "unrecoverable," according to current and former U.S. officials.

top.title: Earthquake kills 8 in Afghanistan, tremors felt across South Asia
top.description: A magnitude 5.9 quake centered in the Hindu Kush region caused a house to collapse on the outskirts of Kabul, killing an entire family.

top.title: Three charged over arson attack on Jewish ambulances in London
top.description: The defendants are due to appear at Westminster Magistrates' Court on Saturday after being arrested on April 1.

top.title: Pentagon logs 365 troops wounded in Operation Epic Fury
top.description: The Defense Department added the Iran campaign to its official casualty database on Friday, providing the most detailed public accounting of the war's toll.

top.title: War silences Good Friday in Jerusalem as Pope Leo XIV calls for peace
top.description: Only a handful of worshippers walked the Via Dolorosa as Israeli restrictions blocked access to holy sites amid the U.S.-Israel war on Iran.

top.title: Trump signs memo ordering back pay for all DHS employees
top.description: The directive extends relief beyond TSA workers to roughly 220,000 federal employees who have gone without pay during the 48-day partial shutdown.

top.title: Justice Alito was secretly hospitalized in March, court confirms
top.description: The 76-year-old Supreme Court justice was treated for dehydration after falling ill at a Federalist Society dinner in Philadelphia.

top.title: Oracle faces backlash over H-1B visa filings amid mass layoffs
top.description: Critics call it a "slap in the face" that the company filed over 3,100 foreign-worker visa petitions in the same period it cut up to 30,000 jobs.

top.title: Satellite images confirm Iran damaged $136M US radar in Saudi Arabia
top.description: New imagery shows charring and missing sections on a THAAD radar antenna struck at Prince Sultan Air Base on March 1.

top.title: 23 state AGs sue to block Trump's mail voting order
top.description: The coalition filed suit Friday in Boston, arguing the executive order unconstitutionally strips states of their authority to administer elections.

top.title: IDF says disarming Hezbollah 'not a goal' of Lebanon campaign
top.description: A senior military official's remarks signal a shift from earlier war aims after one month of fighting that has hit over 3,500 targets.

top.title: US adds 178,000 jobs in March, far beating forecasts
top.description: The unemployment rate fell to 4.3% as the labor market rebounded sharply from February's loss of 92,000 jobs.

top.title: Judge reaffirms block on DOJ subpoenas targeting Fed's Powell
top.description: The ruling sets up a likely appeal that could delay Trump's push to replace Powell with Kevin Warsh before Powell's term expires May 15.
